channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Former Deputy President Warns of Legal Risks for Uhuru if Ruto Wins 2027 Election
item.description: Former deputy president Rigathi Gachagua has warned that former president Uhuru Kenyatta could face imprisonment if President William Ruto secures a second term in the 2027 General Election. The warni...

item.title: Petrol Pump Owners Demand Monthly Pricing and Commission Increase Amid Strike Threat
item.description: Petrol pump owners across Pakistan have announced a nationwide strike following failed negotiations with the government over daily fuel pricing mechanisms. The All Pakistan Petrol Pumps Owners Associa...

item.title: Study reveals alarming decline of rare pookila mouse in Queensland
item.description: A three-year study in southeast Queensland revealed concerning declines in the pookila mouse population, despite deploying 10,666 traps. Researchers found only a single pookila, highlighting potential...

item.title: Asake Buys New Maybach as 'In God We Trust' Tour Continues
item.description: Nigerian singer Asake has added a new Maybach to his luxury car collection, confirming the purchase on Instagram as his 'In God We Trust' world tour continues. The acquisition was confirmed by both th...

item.title: GOP Rep. Burchett criticizes Smithsonian's historical narrative approach
item.description: House Republican Tim Burchett (R-TN) sparked controversy by publicly challenging Smithsonian history director Anthea Hartig during a congressional hearing. The session, held under the House Oversight ...

item.title: PAGASA forecasts potential tropical depression Kiyapo near Philippine Area of Responsibility
item.description: A low pressure area (LPA) outside the Philippine Area of Responsibility (PAR) has a high chance of developing into a tropical depression within 24 hours, according to the Philippine Atmospheric, Geoph...

item.title: Zanzibar raises daladala fares amid rising operating costs and electric bus rollout
item.description: The Revolutionary Government of Zanzibar (RGZ) has raised public transport fares by an average of 16% effective August 1, 2026, coinciding with the launch of electric buses. The minimum daladala fare ...

item.title: Cape Town Warns Residents About Housing Scams on Social Media
item.description: The City of Cape Town’s Human Settlements Directorate has issued a warning to residents about fraudulent housing scams circulating on social media platforms like WhatsApp. Fraudsters are falsely claim...

item.title: IDAC Head Andrea Johnson Denies Influence Over Feroz Khan Amid Murray Murder Allegations
item.description: Advocate Andrea Johnson, head of the Investigating Directorate Against Corruption (IDAC), testified before the Madlanga Commission that she maintained a personal relationship with Crime Intelligence d...

item.title: Matibag Testimony Highlights Limited Fireworks in Controversial Hearing
item.description: The article discusses the impeachment trial of former President Duterte, focusing on the testimony of Melvin Matibag, a key figure in the case. Despite being described as 'explosive' by some, the test...

item.title: AI Robotics Advances: Key Developments in AI, Robotics, and Autonomous Systems
item.description: This article highlights significant advancements in artificial intelligence and robotics, including Samsung's new robotics division, AGIBOT's potential IPO, and Nvidia's open-source world model. It al...

item.title: Ancient DNA Unveils Genetic Links and Social Structure of 4,000-Year-Old Chinese City
item.description: A groundbreaking study published in Nature has used ancient DNA analysis to reveal critical insights into the social organization and genetic heritage of Shimao, a 4,000-year-old Neolithic settlement ...

item.title: Novo Nordisk Files Lawsuit Against Eli Lilly Over Weight-Loss Ad Claims
item.description: Novo Nordisk has filed a lawsuit against Eli Lilly in a U.S. federal court, alleging that Lilly's advertising for its weight-loss drugs is deceptive. The Danish pharmaceutical company claims that Lill...

item.title: Inmates Graduate from Yale Prison Program, Highlighting Educational Reform
item.description: Seven inmates at a Connecticut prison have become the first graduates of a joint Yale and University of New Haven education program, marking a historic milestone in correctional education. Launched in...

item.title: FDA Continues Cyclospora Investigation on Taylor Farms Lettuce Despite False Positive Test
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) confirmed that a previous false positive test result for cyclospora in Taylor Farms lettuce does not alter its ongoing investigation into the outbreak. Desp...

item.title: Nigerian lawmakers push for action on food safety to curb preventable deaths
item.description: Nigerian lawmakers have raised alarm over the alarming number of annual deaths caused by unsafe food, calling for urgent government intervention. The motion highlights that Nigeria records approximate...

item.title: Reevaluating AI's Impact on Employment: China's Role vs. Sinophobic Policies
item.description: The article explores the growing debate around artificial intelligence (AI) and its impact on employment, challenging the common narrative that AI will destroy jobs or be dominated by a single expensi...

item.title: Donald Kaberuka's impact on African economic development
item.description: Donald Kaberuka, a Rwandan refugee who fled persecution in 1959, has become a key figure in shaping Africa's economy over three decades. After earning a Master's in development studies from the Univer...

item.title: Top 2026 World Cup Players By Instagram Follower Growth
item.description: Erling Haaland, the Norway striker, topped the Instagram follower count for the 2026 FIFA World Cup with a massive 32.1 million new followers. His seven goals and joint third place in the Golden Boot ...

item.title: Sean McCann to Compete for Scotland in Commonwealth Games
item.description: Sean McCann, the younger brother of Madeleine McCann, has been selected to compete for Scotland at the upcoming Commonwealth Games in Glasgow. The 21-year-old swimmer will participate in freestyle eve...
